system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error message is:

```
problem while reading header for object physicalProperties
file: .../constant/physicalProperties at line 1.
```

This is a generic "cannot read file" error occurring at line 1, which means the file is either empty, corrupted, or has zero length. This explains why all previous attempts failed — the `physicalProperties` file has been getting corrupted through the editing process, and every attempt to rewrite it has resulted in an empty or malformed file.

The stack trace confirms it's failing at the very first step of reading `physicalProperties`, before even parsing contents.

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Create `constant/physicalProperties` using a robust method that prevents corruption

Instead of using a multi-line editor paste (which can introduce hidden characters or formatting issues across attempts), create the file using a simple shell command that writes the content atom-by-atom:

```bash
cat > constant/physicalProperties << 'EOF'
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    transport       sutherland;
    thermo          janaf;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
}

inertSpecie N2;

species
(
    CH4
    O2
    N2
    H2O
    CO2
);

specie
{
    CH4             16.043;
    O2              31.998;
    N2              28.014;
    H2O             18.015;
    CO2             44.01;
}

energy
{
    h0              0;
    Cp              0;
}

chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;

foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";

foamChemistryThermoFile "<constant>/thermo.compressibleGas";
EOF
```

Execute this single command in the `constant/` directory. This `cat` heredoc method is atomic and immune to the invisible-character issues that have plagued all previous attempts.

### 2. Similarly refresh `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`

The `thermo.compressibleGas` file may also have accumulated corruption. Refresh it with the same atomic approach:

```bash
cat > constant/thermo.compressibleGas << 'EOF'
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    transport       sutherland;
    thermo          janaf;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
}

specie
{
    CH4             16.043;
    O2              31.998;
    N2              28.014;
    H2O             18.015;
    CO2             44.01;
}
EOF
```

### 3. Verify file existence and permissions

After creating the files, verify they exist and are non-empty:

```bash
ls -la constant/
wc -l constant/physicalProperties constant/thermo.compressibleGas
```

Both should show positive line counts. Then check the first few lines:

```bash
head -n 5 constant/physicalProperties
```

If done correctly, the files will contain the exact content shown above with no leading garbage.

### Summary

The fundamental issue across all 6 attempts is that the `physicalProperties` file has been getting corrupted (likely due to copy-paste operations introducing hidden/non-ASCII characters). The `cat` heredoc method writes the file byte-for-byte without any intermediate processing. Combined with refreshing `thermo.compressibleGas` at the same time, this should finally allow OpenFOAM to successfully parse the `physicalProperties` header.</reviewer_analysis>

<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.1 m/s and velocity of air is -0.2 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
